threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'tube' when everyone knows it's all about the Overground here. That's a rookie mistake!
Caught the N109 last night, and a bloke tried to haggle with the driver for a free ride—said he only needed to go a stop. Driver just laughed.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted the same guy in my kiez, fixing shoes under the bridge every Saturday, it hit me: this is home, full of character and quirks.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you find a bar where they serve craft beer from a vintage VW van parked inside. The vibe is as quirky as the locals.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate the dodgy corners—knowing which taco truck's got the best al pastor on a Wednesday makes all the difference.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy feeding his dog a burrito on the 24-Divisadero. Muni is the only place where breakfast tacos and doggie brunch collide.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the tiny plazas where locals gather with their tascas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They're the ones still asking for extra pa amb tomàquet at every meal, thinking they’ve discovered a local secret.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Overground' instead of just 'the train.' Locals know the real vibe is that it's more than a route.
Last night on the 59 by Brixton Market, a girl started handing out homemade cupcakes. Only in South London, right? They were surprisingly good.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One afternoon, I saw three generations of families sharing a kebab stand while kids played nearby. That’s when it hit me—this place is alive.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You'll see folks in Kreuzberg braving the cold with outdoor karaoke setups. It's not just a party; it's a midwinter tradition that keeps the spirit alive.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they don’t flinch at the Muni delays but can rattle off three taco spots in the same breath.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, saw a guy serenading commuters with a ukulele at 16th St. BART—only in the Mission can you catch a live show while waiting for the F-line.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell, but it's the local parks like Plaça de la Virreina that have the real vibe. They miss the casual evening life here.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still take photos of the street performers on every corner, oblivious to the city's soundtrack blending in with their daily lives.
